title: cornus capitata subsp. brevipedunculata
intro: Cornus capitata subsp. brevipedunculata, also known as the Himalayan dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to the mountainous regions of Asia, particularly in the Himalayas. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities that may help reduce swelling and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Cornus capitata subsp. brevipedunculata include its potential to support cardiovascular health, lower blood pressure, and act as an antidiabetic agent. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and decoctions, are used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
